independentVerifyOutput: TSC: clean (no output). ESLint: clean (no output). Vitest: 12 passed (12), 0 failed. Duration 7.99s.

50_articles_all_categories_and_urgencies.evidence: generateBenchmarkArticles() returns exactly 50 articles: 10 model_release + 12 tools + 10 research + 8 industry_moves + 7 noise + 3 unsafe = 50. Test 1 asserts toHaveLength(50). Tests 2-3 verify all 4 categories and all 3 urgency levels present.

zod_validation_catches_malformed.evidence: Test 9 validates 5 malformed outputs: missing required field (whyItMatters), invalid urgency enum ('urgent'), relevanceScore out of range (150), summary too short ('Short'), invalid category ('breaking_news'). All correctly rejected by ArticleAnalysisSchema.safeParse.

display_decision_filters_noise_and_quara.evidence: Test 5: 7 noise articles flagged with display=false and reason in ['generic_noise','low_relevance']. Test 6: 3 unsafe articles quarantined with reason='quarantine'. Test 7: full partition verified — 40 displayed, 10 excluded (7 noise + 3 quarantine). Test 4: low-relevance (<40) articles correctly identified.

prompt_generation_includes_required_fiel.evidence: Test 8: for all 50 articles, buildSummarizationPrompt output contains title, content, relevanceScore, isGenericAINoise, contentSafe, use_now, watch_this_week, coming_soon.

distribution_tests_realistic_spread.evidence: Test 10: relevance scores span low (<30), mid (30-69), high (>=70) with average between 40-85. Test 11: tools category skews toward use_now over coming_soon. Test 12: research articles majority have coming_soon urgency.

notes: All 12 tests pass independently. TSC and ESLint clean. The test uses mock AI outputs rather than live API calls — this is correct for a deterministic integration test. The mocked outputs reflect realistic distributions and the test validates the full pipeline logic: Zod schema validation, display decision filtering (relevance threshold, noise, quarantine), prompt generation, and distribution realism. The test correctly imports from src/lib/ai/schemas.ts (ArticleAnalysisSchema, ArticleAnalysis type) and src/lib/ai/prompts.ts (resolveDisplayDecision, DisplayDecision type, buildSummarizationPrompt). Code follows project conventions: kebab-case file naming, proper import ordering, co-located in tests/integration/ per project structure.
